rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by tracing invalid references and either correcting them or removing the clause.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are either visible features or properly declared within the same annex.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees and assumptions must reference valid expressions with known types; avoid using undefined or improperly typed identifiers.
AGREE helper declarations (eq, const) should align types with their expressions to prevent type mismatch errors.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports or features in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement and assume/guarantee syntax
Component type vs implementation scope distinctions
